ddls:
CREATE TABLE "schools" (
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school */,
  "school_name" TEXT /* Name of the school */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the school's location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the school's location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the school */,
  "school_category" TEXT /* Category of the school (e.g., Public School, Independent School, StrongStart BC) */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the school */,
  "fax" TEXT /* Fax number of the school */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the school */,
  "total_enrollment" INTEGER /* Total number of students enrolled */,
  "num_teachers" INTEGER /* Number of teachers at the school */,
  "num_students_male" INTEGER /* Number of male students */,
  "num_students_female" INTEGER /* Number of female students */,
  "num_students_ethnicity" TEXT /* Number of students by ethnicity */,
  "num_classrooms" INTEGER /* Number of classrooms */,
  "num_libraries" INTEGER /* Number of libraries */,
  "num_sports_facilities" INTEGER /* Number of sports facilities */,
  "accreditation_status" TEXT /* Accreditation status of the school */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website URL of the school */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "school_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "accreditation_requirements" TEXT /* Accreditation requirements for the category */,
  "funding_source" TEXT /* Primary funding source for the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "school_administrators" (
  "admin_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each administrator */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school the administrator is associated with */,
  "admin_name" TEXT /* Full name of the administrator */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the administrator */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the administrator (e.g., Principal, Vice Principal) */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the administrator */,
  "office_location" TEXT /* Office location of the administrator */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the administrator was hired */,
  "highest_degree" TEXT /* Highest degree obtained by the administrator */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Years of experience of the administrator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("admin_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_school_administrators_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES school_admin_roles ("role_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_school_administrators_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "school_admin_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "responsibilities" TEXT /* Main responsibilities of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school the student is enrolled in */,
  "student_name" TEXT /* Full name of the student */,
  "grade" INTEGER /* Grade level of the student */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the student */,
  "home_phone" TEXT /* Home phone number of the student */,
  "parent_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the parent/guardian */,
  "parent_email" TEXT /* Email address of the parent/guardian */,
  "home_address" TEXT /* Home address of the student */,
  "health_conditions" TEXT /* Health conditions of the student */,
  "allergies" TEXT /* Allergies of the student */,
  "behavior_records" TEXT /* Behavior records of the student */,
  "disciplinary_actions" TEXT /* Disciplinary actions taken for the student */,
  "total_absences" INTEGER /* Total number of absences */,
  "total_tardies" INTEGER /* Total number of tardies */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_grade FOREIGN KEY ("grade") REFERENCES grades ("grade_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grades" (
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grade level */,
  "grade_name" TEXT /* Name of the grade level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the grade level */,
  "curriculum" TEXT /* Curriculum followed in the grade level */,
  "standards" TEXT /* Academic standards for the grade level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("grade_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "student_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student */,
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* ID of the grade level */,
  "score" REAL /* Academic score of the student */,
  "attendance" REAL /* Attendance record of the student */,
  "assessment_type" TEXT /* Type of assessment (e.g., test, project, quiz) */,
  "date_of_assessment" TEXT /* Date of the assessment */,
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the teacher who assessed the student */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_performance_teacher_id FOREIGN KEY ("teacher_id") REFERENCES teachers ("teacher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_performance_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_performance_grade_id FOREIGN KEY ("grade_id") REFERENCES grades ("grade_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teachers" (
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each teacher */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school the teacher is employed by */,
  "teacher_name" TEXT /* Full name of the teacher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the teacher */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject taught by the teacher */,
  "home_phone" TEXT /* Home phone number of the teacher */,
  "personal_email" TEXT /* Personal email address of the teacher */,
  "highest_degree" TEXT /* Highest degree obtained by the teacher */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Years of experience of the teacher */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Certification of the teacher */,
  "classes_taught" TEXT /* List of classes taught by the teacher */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("teacher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teachers_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each class */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school offering the class */,
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* ID of the grade level */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject of the class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the class */,
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the teacher teaching the class */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of students in the class */,
  "enrollment" INTEGER /* Current number of students in the class */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_classes_teacher_id FOREIGN KEY ("teacher_id") REFERENCES teachers ("teacher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_classes_grade_id FOREIGN KEY ("grade_id") REFERENCES grades ("grade_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_classes_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "class_schedules" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each schedule record */,
  "class_id" INTEGER /* ID of the class */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of the class */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the class */,
  "room" TEXT /* Room where the class is held */,
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the teacher teaching the class */,
  "day_of_week" TEXT /* Day of the week the class is held */,
  "semester" TEXT /* Semester the class is offered (e.g., Fall, Spring) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_class_schedules_class_id FOREIGN KEY ("class_id") REFERENCES classes ("class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_class_schedules_teacher_id FOREIGN KEY ("teacher_id") REFERENCES teachers ("teacher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Administrator, Teacher, Student) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
